_meta.description: Wave 6 finance posting, valuation, and plant-performance projection governance policy.

objective.name: Finance Posting, Valuation, And Plant Performance Truth
objective.statement: Every period close, backdate exception, correction memo, valuation snapshot, and plant-performance snapshot must exist for a concrete financial or operational control purpose, preserve auditable source lineage, and never be published as an editable second truth when it is only a projection or derived read model.

build_questions.question: Which close control, posting decision, valuation checkpoint, or plant KPI review is this object born to serve, and what false financial or operational truth appears if it does not exist?
Is this object a first-class finance gate owner such as period close, backdate exception, or governed memo, or is it only a projection that must remain read-only?
If this record is a valuation or snapshot projection, why would manual create, update, delete, or transition ever be allowed, and what downstream KPI distortion would that permit?
Does the record preserve snapshot time, source system, source record, and organization scope so an auditor can trace the value or KPI back to a real source event?
Do the canonical relationships cover the real subject types used in runtime, or is a dynamic control still pretending that only one invoice or one source path exists?
For posting-period and correction controls, are reason, approval, signature meaning, and expiry enforced at the backend rather than left as optional notes?
Can plant OEE, quality, delivery, and valuation metrics be regenerated from governed projections without allowing a user to rewrite history directly in the KPI table?
Can the backend prove from one chain of records why a period was closed, reopened, adjusted, valued, and reported, with enough evidence for auto-audit and management review?
If a finance or analytics element adds no unique control purpose, has it been isolated as archive or unused instead of left live as a second reporting truth?
